1047.reason: This condition incorrectly restricts the search to only tokens on the same line. If the assignment operator '=' appears on a different line (e.g., due to a newline in the source code), this function fails to detect it, causing the parser to misinterpret the statement as an expression statement without assignment.

788.reason: According to the grammar, the expression inside braces is optional ([ Exp { ',' Exp } ]). Unconditionally calling parseExp() will fail to parse empty initialization lists like '{}' and cause incorrect token consumption or parsing errors.

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  [Error] The grammar allows empty initialization '{ }', but this unconditionally calls parseExp(), which will incorrectly consume the '}' token or cause a parsing error when the braces are empty.
